what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet that defines layout, visual styling, and component states for a Virtual Hub full-bleed photo viewer overlay, covering the stage background, brand bug, hub switcher, bottom menu, quick menu, info panel, share panel, floorplan overlay, capture/comment popup, and tap marker.
observations: Heavily relies on CSS custom properties for color, typography, radii, and shadows.
Uses glassmorphism via rgba backgrounds and backdrop-filter blur across many panels.
Stage background is a warm architectural-render placeholder built from layered radial and linear gradients, with pseudo-elements for a floor line and a glass column shadow.
UI components are absolutely positioned over a full-bleed stage.
Active states use a gold accent color and pill-shaped treatments.
Floorplan overlay includes an animated pulsing dashed ring for the active dot.
Capture popup includes comment skeleton rows, attachment placeholders, a message input toolbar, a status pill, and a pager.
Vignette uses pointer-events: none so it does not block interaction.
Uses aspect-ratio for floorplan canvas and capture image placeholders.
Z-index values range from 8 to 12 without an explicit stacking-context document.
standouts: The warm architectural-render placeholder is created entirely with CSS gradients and pseudo-elements.
Extensive glassmorphic panels share a consistent rgba(31,26,18,.7/.92) surface treatment.
The capture-comment popup bundles a photo preview, comments, attachments, message input, status, and pager in one component.
conventions: Class names use a vh- prefix and BEM-like block-element structure.
CSS custom properties are used consistently for theming tokens.
Labels and buttons often use uppercase text-transform and wide letter-spacing.
Spacing and sizing follow a 4/8/12/14/16/22/24 px scale.
Active and hover states are handled with background/color transitions.
concerns: Stage background is a hardcoded placeholder gradient rather than a real image asset.
No media queries or responsive adjustments for the fixed 360px info panel or 720px floorplan card.
Z-index values are scattered and not documented as a coordinated stacking scale.
Some colors use raw rgba values while others use CSS variables, creating inconsistency.
The .vh-bug-logo .a selector references an unexplained class 'a'.
The tap marker is pointer-events: none with no click/hover affordance defined.
No reduced-motion handling for the floorplan pulse animation.
notes: This is the styling layer for a luxury, photo-first virtual hub viewer, using glassmorphic overlays and warm architectural gradients over a full-bleed stage.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties such as --font-body, --accent-gold, and --bg-surface defined?
What is the intended real image asset to replace the placeholder architectural-render gradient?
How is the hub switcher active state managed at runtime?
What determines the absolute positioning of the capture popup on the stage?
Is there a global z-index scale coordinating these overlays?
Does the design support light mode, or is it dark-only?
What does the .a class represent inside .vh-bug-logo?
How is floorplan room/dot data generated or bound?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles overlay UI components over a full-bleed photo stage.
apparent_themes: Subtle motion feedback for active states
intention_signals: To build a polished virtual tour hub where UI floats above a large photograph, letting users switch hubs, open menus, view property info, share, inspect a floorplan, and annotate captures with comments.
novelty: The file combines a capture-comment popup with attachment placeholders, status pill, pager, and an animated floorplan active-dot pulse within one cohesive overlay CSS kit, alongside a warm architectural-render placeholder gradient.
contribution: Provides a complete visual vocabulary and layout rules for an overlay-style virtual hub interface, including glass-panel surface tokens, warm gradient stage treatment, and component states that a fused system could reuse for immersive media viewers.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet for the Virtual Hub viewer kit that lays out glassmorphic overlay UI components atop a full-bleed warm architectural photo stage.
intention_for_embedding: To define the visual styling and component states for an immersive photo-first virtual hub with hub switching, info/share panels, floorplan overlay, and capture annotation.
